reformed_silver_council_reform.name: Reformed Silver Council
reformed_silver_council_reform.description: The republic, ancient as it is, has gone through a considerable amount of reform in recent years, being improved and extending its authority farther to all under the Mother’s shadow

eordand_moon_republic_reform.name: Moon Republic
eordand_moon_republic_reform.description: Finding its origins in the merchant republic of Bagcatir, the Moon Republic has since evolved into something much more. With its robust constitution guaranteeing rights and representation to disparate faiths and locales, and a strong focus on trade and commerce, the Moon Republic stands as a pluralistic and tolerant force in the normally fractious Eordand. As they now say, all are truly equal under the glow of the Moon.

racial_tolerance_reform.name: Racial Tolerance
racial_tolerance_reform.description: All sentient creatures are free and equal in dignity and rights. By enshrining those ideas in the Constitution of our Republic we shall further our strive for democracy, freedom and equality. Every person regardless of race shall be permitted to serve in even the highest offices of state.

forge_council_reform.name: The Forge Council
forge_council_reform.description: The Forge Council is made up of the ten Forge-Clans, with five representatives from each forge clan elected to the council. There are four unofficial but prominent blocs within the Forge Council: The Stalwarts, Schemers, Militants, and Colonists. Each clan usually falls within one of these general tendencies. Any member of the Council is eligible to be elected Kylakas if they garner sufficient support, though the common practice is for each bloc to prop up one of their own, so that their interests may be served.

the_stone_jun.name: The Stone Jun
the_stone_jun.description: The Stone Army, a mercenary company, rules Gushuokguan as a stratocracy. Most members of the Stone Army are natives of Gushuokguan who have bled and fought for the realm, and are seen by the populace as the legitimate rulers of the realm. Most affairs are dealt with by local village councils with the eunuchs controlling no more than the dwelling of Gushibi besides handling diplomatic matters.

gongmin_lianbang.name: Gongmin Lianbang
gongmin_lianbang.description: Our tradition of veteran rule has slowly evolved into the "democracy of the warrior". Men who have taken part in at least one campaign are given voting rights. Those who have served in the Stone Armies for five years or more have the right to hold office and be elected to the Veterans Council, which represents the constituencies of the republic.

kobildzan_draconic_technopriests.name: Draconic Technopriests
kobildzan_draconic_technopriests.description: The dragon-priests, having long been the class which held the bonds of kobold society together, have ascended to total power in Kobildzan... at least, sort of. Dragon-priests are found at each level of societal organization, but they now jockey for power with technocratic artificers. In some ways, this leads to the field of invention and religion becoming one; in others, the rivalries between the two are fierce and contested. Everywhere, though, the government is made of both dragon-priests and technocrats.

kobildzan_draconic_elites.name: Draconic Elites
kobildzan_draconic_elites.description: Kobolds have become dragons: at least, some of them have. The title of "dragon" is reserved for those of the elite rung of society: the artificers, dragon-priests, officers, and bureaucrats who have earned their place at the top. These elites have been given the title of "Dragon," allowing them access to all the newest and finest additions of Ascension. The literal evolution of the elites of society has allowed for incredible stratification and centralization of power.

kobildzan_draconic_masses.name: Draconic Masses
kobildzan_draconic_masses.description: There is no distinction between a kobold and a dragon. The masses all have rights to become draconic, and the nation of Kobildzan ensures that everyone may Ascend, no matter their stature. Though it may lead to odd situations– like a gnome with a few scales calling herself a "dragon," and having the same draconic rights as a kobold who stands twenty feet tall– it ensures that all have their chance to truly evolve. The masses can become dragons!

floodborn_council_reform_republics.name: Floodborn Council
floodborn_council_reform_republics.description: The Floodborn of Wyvernheart have been assembled into a grand council which directs the nation's administration and votes on decisions to present to the Sovereign who has the final say in all matters.

ikogshaantus_assembly_of_guilds.name: Assembly of Guilds
ikogshaantus_assembly_of_guilds.description: For the purpose of assisting our realm in industrialization, an assembly has been organized to advise our ruler and manage economic matters. But unlike other nations, the assembly is not composed of the rich and powerful of the realm, who only serve their own interests. Instead, the assembly is staffed by the leaders of the Sanadazjal, the most skillful and intelligent artisans and engineers who are the most dedicated to Enuuk.

ikogshaantus_khantaar_industrial_council.name: Rünsukhi Industrial Council
ikogshaantus_khantaar_industrial_council.description: It has been proven again and again: the Sanadazjal are the true representatives of the great Goddess Enuuk. The more they proliferate throughout our realm, the closer we come to our desired perfection. At last, the very dynamic of power in our nation has come to be defined by the will of the Sanadazjal. Unlike a traditional republican structure, the highest decisions are made by a council of the most important guild leaders, each representing the interests of various industries.

decentralized_luna_confederation_reform.name: Decentralised Luna Confederation
decentralized_luna_confederation_reform.description: The sibling-cities of the Luna possess a gaggle of overlapping institutions into which the individual treasuries of these wealthy polities contribute. The flexible reserve funds of such collaborative endeavours can be placed into mutual defence, development and infrastructure, though a healthy amount of argument naturally accompanies any investment decision.

centralized_luna_confederation_reform.name: Centralised Luna Confederation
centralized_luna_confederation_reform.description: It would be a mistake to say the many parallel institutions of the Luna city states have been truly tamed, but they now pull in the same direction and are subject to audit by confederate Parliament. Customs and excise are united across the valley, and the profusion of debate and delay has at least been focused down to office complexes within walking distance of one another.

free_city_of_luna.name: Free City of Luna
free_city_of_luna.description: This is a free Imperial city that has been granted special rights and privileges by the Emperor himself.

the_mazukxiem.name: The Mazukxiem
the_mazukxiem.description: An unconventional Republic, the Mazukxiem is a combination of three gnoll packs, all claiming descent from Brrtekuh in some way and all represented by a pack master who has proven themself through a set of trials. The Ekxiemgo, the Council Master who has proven themselves through a combination of trials devised by each of the packs, is expected to lead them all, though they are still beholden to the group and expected to step down when need be. As a precautionary method to keep top state secrets as well as preserve experience, office holders have to be eaten, willingly or not, should they fail to secure their position.

xedaxhaz.name: Xedaxhaz
xedaxhaz.description: Xedaxhaz, the 'many-pack', is the term for a collection of documented policies and systems constructed by the packs of Brrtekuh. They aim to reinforce the balance between the many factions within the government that seek power for themselves, as well as motivate cooperative endeavours between the normally domineering gnolls. Scrap-shotel duels at dawn, salvage-sharing arrangements, marriage and bone-marrow pacts alike - if it help tie Brrtekuh together, it goes in the Xedaxhaz.

blackpowder_commonwealth_republic.name: Blackpowder Commonwealth
blackpowder_commonwealth_republic.description: Pashainéy representation is conceptualised in blocs, emerging from the guild representation system which dominated the city-republics of the pre-Pashainé Luna. Constituency seats representing one’s locality are combined with parliamentary seats for major industries, consultative groups and species; a relatively powerful Parliamentary executive is constrained by highly influential confidence votes. The result is that a single person may be represented multiple times – for example, as a resident of a given city, as a textile guild-member, as a kobold, and as a renter.

z22_prince_elect.name: The Prince Elect
z22_prince_elect.description: The Republic of Celmaldor commonly elects its Prince or Princess among the most prominent clans of its cities. Despite the power of the Clans most elections also have a popular candidate of no clan affiliation, usually representing the Human powerholders of the republic in their policy.

z22_prince_elect_improved.name: The Prince Elect
z22_prince_elect_improved.description: Recognising flaws and nepotistic inefficencies within the old system the Republic of Celmaldor modernised its elections throughout its history. In the new system the candidates are not restricted to the Merchant Clans, to better represent the interests of the greater Celmaldorian Trade League each candidate may be of any family in the nation so long as they have full citisenship. However, most candidates still have some Clan backing despite these restrictions.

z22_silver_diet.name: The Silver Diet
z22_silver_diet.description: The republic has grown from a city state to a large confederation of towns and greater trading cities. to better administer these locations and to better represent the wishes of their merchants and landholders we shall implement a system of Parliament by which they may have representatives in the central government.

z22_skaldol_treaty_of_faiths.name: Skaldol Treaty of Faith
z22_skaldol_treaty_of_faiths.description: With our Confederation now encompassing a multitude of relgious minorities it has become an issue that commerce is halted by disagreements of faith. This cannot do and so the Confederation and trade League formed a treaty signed at the prominent Skaldhyrric holy site of Skaldol which guaranteed the freedom for each citisen to practice what faith they wish, so long as it not be an evil unto otehrs.

leading_clergy_reform.name: Clerical State
leading_clergy_reform.description: This state is ruled by the clergy and uses the hierarchy of religious institutions for all government purposes.

monastic_order_reform.name: Monastic Order
monastic_order_reform.description: This country is ruled by the members of a Monastic Order. The order's hierarchy and rules replaces the need for most laws and administration.

papacy_reform.name: High Rectorate
papacy_reform.description: A political structure where the nation is governed by a spiritual and intellectual leader, the High Rector, the highest ranking office of the Ravelian Church.\nThe High Rector and the Veridicals have premier access to the God Fragment, and are the first to hear any of its revelations.

wulin.name: Wulin
wulin.description: The holder of the righteous Paths holy site, the Xiadao, and the rightful leader of the Xiaken.\n§MA country can take over the Wulin, and become the overlord of all Xiaken Subjects, by taking the province of Xiadao.§!

one_xia.name: One Xia
one_xia.description: The Xianjie, long divided, has been united. From the seeds of reckless discord a humble unity has sprouted and flourished among the once disparate temples and orders of the Xia. After centuries of conflict the Righteous Masters have united under the sovereignty of the Supreme Master, Protector of the Xiadao, leader of the One Xia, and temporal champion of the righteous Paths faithful.

xia_parliament_reform.name: The Righteous Conclave
xia_parliament_reform.description: The Righteous Summit was an antiquated tradition of the Xia in which all of the Righteous Masters were invited to the Jiangliubao in order to deliberate on the affairs of the Xia. The expansion and centralization of the One Xia has prompted the development of this ancient tradition into a modern institution. Every Temple of the Accretive or Transmutative Path elects from among their masters a delegate - some would say a hostage - that is sent to reside for the rest of their life in the Xiadao compound. The Summit is not completely ended, however, and every decade all Righteous Masters join their temple's delegate in the Righteous Conclave.

xiaken.name: Xiaken
xiaken.description: A community led by martial warriors of the Accretive and Transmutative Path faiths, and nominally subservient to the Wulin.\n§MA country can take over the Wulin, and become the overlord of all Xiaken Subjects, by taking the province of Xiadao.§!

indep_xiaken.name: Independent Xiaken
indep_xiaken.description: A community led by martial warriors of the Accretive and Transmutative Path faiths, and nominally subservient to the Wulin.\n§MA country can take over the Wulin, and become the overlord of all Xiaken Subjects, by taking the province of Xiadao.§!

nirat_priesthood_reform.name: Nirat Priesthood
nirat_priesthood_reform.description: The Nirat Priesthood, who for years were denied their rightful place as rulers of the Sorrow have finally broken free from the Elikhetist yoke and taken over the Stewardship of the Sorrow. They are in control of matters both temporal and divine and hold sway over the realm through religious legitimacy. Where the faith goes, so does the state and where the state goes, so does the faith.

aakheta_ahati_reform.name: Ahati Stewards
aakheta_ahati_reform.description: The time of the Kheteratan Kings has come to an end. No longer will the Sorrow be stewarded by pompous monarchs. Now the Ahati, the vigilant servants of Sorrow and Aakhet will rule over the land, commerce and industry. We will steward our newly formed realm into an era of Peace, Freedom, Justice, and Security! Our allegiance will be to the Sorrow, to Aakhet!

secular_order_reform.name: Secular Order
secular_order_reform.description: Similar to the Monastic Order, but religion plays no part in their governance.

tagharoghi_reform.name: Tagharoghi
tagharoghi_reform.description: The High Council of the Oni, representatives from the four great Shirgrii meet to discuss matters under the observant gaze of the all powerful Rashenbir, who acts as an arbitrator in conflicts between the Shirgrii and has the final word in the direction of the state.

adventurer_reform.name: Adventuring Company
adventurer_reform.description: A loose form of government centered around a large adventuring party, exploration and settling new regions. Uses §YAdventuring Efficiency§W mechanics which simulates the spoils of passive adventurers within the company.

dwarovar_adventurer_reform.name: Dwarovar Adventurer Company
dwarovar_adventurer_reform.description: A loose form of government centered around a large adventuring party, exploration and settling the Dwarovar. Uses §YAdventuring Efficiency§W mechanics which simulates the spoils of passive adventurers within the company.

aelantir_adventurer_reform.name: Aelantir Adventurer Company
aelantir_adventurer_reform.description: A loose form of government centered around a large adventuring party, exploration and settling Aelantir. Uses §YAdventuring Efficiency§W mechanics which simulates the spoils of passive adventurers within the company.

sponsored_adventurer_reform.name: Sponsored Adventurers
sponsored_adventurer_reform.description: The Sponsored Adventurer Company was created not by the will of likeminded glory-seekers, but by deliberate state action.  Partially funded directly by their home nation, the Sponsored Adventurer Company seeks land, power, and control for their people as much as they seek glory and riches.

magisterium_reform.name: Magisterium
magisterium_reform.description: The Magisterium is the ruling body of the official mages within the Empire of Anbennar. They are, as a whole, also the de-facto purveyors and leaders of magical knowledge and learning in all of Cannor.

magocracy_reform.name: Magocracy
magocracy_reform.description: While some countries may be ruled by the nobility or by the merchant class, magocracies derive their prerequisite to rule from the ability to cast magic.

arakeprun_magocracy.name: Arakepruni Magocracy
arakeprun_magocracy.description: The state is controlled by mages working in harmony with scholars, made possible by the uncontested hegemony of the Spring Court.

verkal_ozovar_magocracy.name: The Magocratic Diarchy
verkal_ozovar_magocracy.description: The mountaintop mystics of old give way to the rebirth of Runesmithing, with Verkal Ozovar at its core. The government is led by a council of prominent rune-mages, with the Diarch and the Oz-Amekh at the helm.

oracular_order_reform.name: Oracular Order
oracular_order_reform.description: A monastic order in which the ruling class consists of a group of monks that have dedicated their lives to the study of the mechanics of the world. While they have gained the ability to predict the future, they need to maintain their studies to keep the future in sight.

gerudia_skaldhyrric_order.name: Skaldhyrric Order
gerudia_skaldhyrric_order.description: To train Skalds is a difficult task, involving both the memorization of countless tales and coupling these tales with divine power. Our state has a great responsibility to ensure that all of Gerudia can have access to our high quality Skalds, for we are the only ones who can create more than storytellers. Being the foremost educator of Skalds also has the benefit of allowing us to gather favors that may otherwise be impossible.

mithradhum_ancestor_venerator_reform.name: Ancestral Venerator
mithradhum_ancestor_venerator_reform.description: Aul-Dwarov fell, first and foremost, because of moral and spiritual rot. They failed to venerate the ancestors, venerating their own greed and avarice instead. By birthright and by connection to holy Amldihr, our government is the rightful inheritor of the ancient empire of the dwarves.

elikhander_theocracy_reform.name: Elikhander Hierocracy
elikhander_theocracy_reform.description: Anybody who is anybody in Elikhand can trace their lineage to Kheterata, and their faith towards the Khets. With this shared identity there is little competition between the elites of this nation, for we are one people united by blood and faith. Together we build this nation towards glory and reverence.

infernal_order_state_reform.name: Infernalist Order-State
infernal_order_state_reform.description: Ruled directly by one of the three holy orders of the Infernal Court, the Order-State exists entirely under their absolute and unquestioned authority.

kar_ankash_theocracy_reform.name: Kar-Ankash Council
kar_ankash_theocracy_reform.description: The Kar-Ankash Council is a theocratic council of Black Orc shamans in Hul-Jorkad bent on defending and spreading the teachings of Dookan in their oldest form. They reject chieftains and kings, instead elevating a leader from among the council, a Tarbastan which translates to 'High Old One', when the previous one can no longer fulfil their duties.

balrijin_sage_council_reform.name: Sage Council
balrijin_sage_council_reform.description: The Sage Council is the theocratic council of Balrijin, consisting of the sages of the most powerful or affluent clans, factions, and guilds in the realm. They congregate in the Ri-Iha Khang temple at the top of Balriza to discuss matters of both state and faith, but in the end the Golden Sage, a sage picked among the council, has the final word. The Golden Sage is to interpret the teachings of Balris and apply them as closely as possible. If they become unable to fulfill their duties, their Protégé, who is picked from among the current sages, will take their place.

land_of_thousand_shrines.name: Land of Thousand Shrines
land_of_thousand_shrines.description: The state has been entirely supplanted by Liho's Maidens, who now exercise absolute control over this country and its domains. Leadership consists exclusively of the most magically gifted - and politically connected - shrine maidens in the order.

husziens_council.name: Huszien's Council
husziens_council.description: The Huli-Jing and the Gumiho have assembled into two large factions, vying with one another for influence over Huszien's decisions. As either side gains power, the organs of state of which they are patrons grow in power, but lose much of their effectiveness from inefficiency.

holy_matriarchy_reform.name: Holy Matriarchy
holy_matriarchy_reform.description: The city of Lelanàn is ruled by priestesses dedicated to worship of the goddess Lel, leader of the halfling pantheon. After the Kolo’s temple on the mainland Sarhal was lost to human migration, the Lel’s temple of Lelanàn assumed the leading position among the shrines and thus became the most important place of Nakavy Avo faith.

haraagtsedi_league_reform.name: Haraagtsedi League
haraagtsedi_league_reform.description: Haraagtseda is a collection of packs no longer, we are a true nation! Our Mazla Haraag will be decided by a test of skill open to all of the holy men and women of our realm. They will not rule alone but be advised by the Gravot, an assembly of representatives from each of the provinces. Unlike our kin to the west we will not be ruled by tyrants, each Gnoll, and some humans, will be able to vote for those who represent them

shadow_horde.name: Shadow Horde
shadow_horde.description: While the trolls of Yezel Mora are ostensibly under the control of the three Shadow Hags, in reality they tend to leave the running of the horde to the trolls themselves. The hag's main involvement is to choose a new Nail King when the previous one dies. Otherwise they trust the king to rally support, train the military, collect taxes, and keep order. Should the Nail King fail in these tasks however, the hags will deliver swift and cruel punishment, before convening to choose a new ruler.

irkorzik_dunesworn_council_reform.name: Dunesworn Council
irkorzik_dunesworn_council_reform.description: Founded by Irkorzik with the support of the Vog Mekza, our pack inheritance is now determined through the Trials of the Sands. Our current pack leader elects a candidate, and then the Dunesworn Council chooses a lethal trial that the candidate must pass in the Salahad. If they succeed, they become the next heir of our pack.

raithtall_twilight_court_reform.name: Twilight Court
raithtall_twilight_court_reform.description: A theocracy in service to the Gloam Knight and the Autumn Court. While officially led by the revived priesthood of Dorcurt, the old royal dynasty of Raithtall still wields considerable influence within the Court.

internal_mission_reform.name: Internal Mission
internal_mission_reform.description: We must focus our energy on our mission within our country, to protect the faithful, feed the poor and fulfill our religious duties.

external_mission_reform.name: External Mission
external_mission_reform.description: The spread of our holy faith must be our foremost mission and we cannot rest until we have defeated all enemies of our religion.

mission_to_civilize_reform.name: Mission to Civilize
mission_to_civilize_reform.description: Our mission is to seek out poor misguided primitives across the world and teach them the proper way to align their lives so that they too may join us in our divinity.

mission_to_kill_pirates_reform.name: Mission on the High Seas
mission_to_kill_pirates_reform.description: The seas are filled with heathens and villains plundering the trade and transport ships of the honest followers of our faith. It’s become clear that pilgrims will never be safe to visit the holy places under our protection if we do not make it our holy mission to eliminate the piratical menace on the high seas!

balanced_mission_reform.name: Balanced Mission
balanced_mission_reform.description: The affairs of our theocracy cannot be focused on only one aspect. It is in the best interest of both our state and our faith to invest equally in our internal and external missions.

commercial_mission_reform.name: Commercial Mission
commercial_mission_reform.description: Although religion is the driving force of our ambitions, it is undeniable that our state is dependent on the might of the Ducat. Fortunately, religion and monetization go hand in hand.

mission_to_protect_reform.name: Mission of Protection
mission_to_protect_reform.description: Our faith is at constant risk from heathens and heretics alike. The only ones who we can count on are other followers of our religion - our brothers and sisters in faith. It is our duty to protect them from the godforsaken forces of evil.

exploring_the_frontiers.name: Exploring the Frontier
exploring_the_frontiers.description: "To see where you stand, you must leave it behind." -Narawen the Wanderer\n\nWe have maps and charts of old Escann aplenty, but the one before us has been reshaped by the Greentide, and we will need to learn it anew (while keeping up with the roaming goblin and orcish warbands). By ensuring we have well-built wagons, and that every member can set up and take down camp within minutes, we will speed the company on its way and Escann on its regrowth accordingly.

growing_the_band.name: Stripping the Land
growing_the_band.description: "You either further the cause, or oppose it." -Sir Laurens síl Place\n\nWe seek to recreate a new Escann out of the ashes of the old. But that will not be an easy process, and we will have to lean heavily on the land to support its rebuilding. We cannot leave a single stone unturned, resource unexploited, or "lost" treasure unfound, if we are to succeed in our quest.

the_glorious_quest.name: Pioneer Mission
the_glorious_quest.description: "Good adventurers think of tactics, great adventurers think of logistics." -Aucan síl Versil\n\nMost adventurers lent the Marcher Lords their sword and shield thinking of glory and loot, confident in their ability to take on orc and goblin. But on the 11th of Nerament, 1444, they woke up faced with a quest most were unprepared for: rebuilding nations. Just as we would study a dragon before slaying one, we must immediately get to work on an organized code of conduct and all the other pieces of administration we will need in the months, years, and decades to come.

corintar_corinite_crusade.name: Corinite Crusade
corintar_corinite_crusade.description: Some states vary in their purpose, adjusting to the whims of a monarch or the rise of a new family of patricians. But not the Corintar. Our purpose is clear, our cause Corin's own, and every aspect of our state will be geared to Her crusade.

dwarovar_serpentspine_spelunking.name: Serpentspine Spelunking
dwarovar_serpentspine_spelunking.description: Wanderlust is usually reserved for humans and their never-ending greed to find more and achieve more. But when it comes to our ancient homeland we have every right to discover what was was once ours, and we have every right to enjoy it! Take pride, smile and laugh as you would in a tavern. We may have lost our home, but we have been granted a great boon in our lifetime, the opportunity to see it with fresh eyes. Every cave, every crevice, every lost hold is history of our forefathers to be found!

dwarovar_call_to_the_surfacers.name: Call to the Surfacers
dwarovar_call_to_the_surfacers.description: The Greentide is broken! The Dwarovar is open! Come all my kin and answer my cry. It is time to come home, children of Balgar, children of Hehogrim, children of Dagrin Everbeard! We are all dwarves, and we must all do our part to find our way home at long last.

dwarovar_remnants_of_old.name: Remnants of Old
dwarovar_remnants_of_old.description: The Dwarovar still stands! We feared that every remnant of the dwarven empire had been razed to bare rock by the Blacktide, but we have discovered that a rare few holds have survived, fortified within their great citadels! These ancient remnants of Aul-Dwarov have become stagnant and listless with the passage of centuries, but in many ways they are our ancestors now living among us, and it is our duty to awaken them!

shamanistic_prosletysm.name: Shamanistic Prosletysm
shamanistic_prosletysm.description: We have until now tolerated heathens within our state; their worship did little to interfere with our proceedings. However, as some voices within our Shaman circles have pointed out, we have our own cults for these people, lay clergy from whom they can seek their spiritual guidance. So let’s bring them into our fold, they can worship their gods within our Temples and their gods can be led by our Shamans - after all, our Gods are theirs too.

appease_khet_theocracy.name: Appease the Khet
appease_khet_theocracy.description: Our state is technically not the owner of the lands of the Sorrow, technically it is just a steward which manages the lands and river in the name of the Khet. The Khet, feline-human hybrids, demi-gods, and true owners of the lands and river have named us the stewards. This gives us the right to subjugate and control the lands of the Salahad and the authority to do as we please, as long as we praise, worship and serve the Khet in any way they please, no matter if it is moral or debaucherous.

sharpening_the_blade_reform.name: Sharpening the Blade
sharpening_the_blade_reform.description: In a hostile and foreign land, the best protection is a sharp blade and a sharper eye. In order to survive, we must continuously fight the uncivilized masses on our borders. To this end, we have focused on having the best trained and fiercest fighting force we can muster.

raising_the_standard_reform.name: Raising the Standard
raising_the_standard_reform.description: Fighting for our very survival in these wilds has led us to amass a vast fighting force.  In order to keep our armies stocked with able-bodied men, we have raised the banners of war for all true subjects to follow.  Our population sees it as their mission to birth the next generation of warriors that protect our ways of life.

education_of_the_bishop_reform.name: Education of the Theocrat
education_of_the_bishop_reform.description: The fate of our state lies in the hands of God. But it is to our theocratic ruler to read the divine signs and interpret them in favor of our people.\nTo avoid misinterpretations, the state must focus its efforts in educating future rulers.

education_of_the_advisors_reform.name: Education of the Court
education_of_the_advisors_reform.description: Our ruler might represent the will of our faith, but he is still just a human prone to human mistakes. With an education of the whole court we will be able to minimize the errors of our ruler.

education_of_the_missionaries_reform.name: Education of the Missionaries
education_of_the_missionaries_reform.description: The heathens and heretics are the greatest tests of our belief. They seduce our pious men with poisonous thoughts, drifting them away from the true faith.\nWe must teach our missionaries about those threats.

education_of_the_people_reform.name: Education of the People
education_of_the_people_reform.description: Knowledge and education are a gift of God. Man is born with free will and thought, and it is our duty to ensure that this gift gets shared with everyone in the state.

disciplined_party_leaders.name: Disciplined Party Leaders
disciplined_party_leaders.description: "It's what you don't and won't do, not what you do, that separates an adventurer from a bandit with delusions of grandeur." - Aucan síl Versil\n\nEvery adventuring party thinks itself unique, facing decisions no one has faced and making choices no one else could - or should - make. All too often, they make the wrong one, such as ransacking a village's winter stores as necessary supplies. We can speak to those parties' leaders with gentle explanations or harsh demands, but if we want this land to grow out of the ashes, we will need to ensure that even the most unruly adventuring parties know that there are rules and laws to follow.

unregulated_looting_practices.name: Unregulated Looting Practices
unregulated_looting_practices.description: "Yes, I know that by some narrow definition of the law, this dagger 'belonged' to you. But I followed a much greater law in taking it: I needed it, to trim my beard." - Rethag Ironbrow\n\nThe Battle for Castonath did not end the desperate war for survival we were waging. It merely changed it. The stakes are simply too high, and petty complaints like 'I needed that horse to plow' or 'that suit of armor was passed down from my great-grandfather' are too insignificant, to prevent our forces from taking what they need, when they need it.

dwarovar_leadership_of_the_clans.name: Leadership of the Clans
dwarovar_leadership_of_the_clans.description: Each dwarf belongs to a clan and that clan, theoretically, looks after their own. Despite living amongst the surfacers all this time, these clans underpin and teach us what it means to be dwarven, preserving the (sometimes rigid) ways of Aul-Dwarov where our people might falter.

dwarovar_reclaimers_of_the_dwarovar.name: Reclaimers of the Dwarovar
dwarovar_reclaimers_of_the_dwarovar.description:
Hark!

Each dwarf has a story of one of their disparate ancestors forming expeditions to reclaim a little piece of the Serpentspine for themselves, and until now, those were mere stories. Parables. Warnings.

But now, as we are in the halls of our ancestors, in the same caves they mined and the same roads they fought on that it can be undeniable that this time we dwarves are here to stay.

dwarovar_cartel_oversight.name: Cartel Oversight
dwarovar_cartel_oversight.description: The Cartels have become a fixture in surfacer economy since our ancestors came down from the mountain all those years ago, entrenching themselves in human society as reliable bankers, traders and craftsmen. While our expedition has no doubt been funded by a cartel one way or the other, we could allow them into our leadership proper if we are truly to flourish in these deep dark tunnels.

ravelian_state_technocracy.name: Ravelian Technocracy
ravelian_state_technocracy.description: A baseline understanding of artifice is considered crucial if one is to advance in the state, or get closer to God

importing_administrators_reform.name: Importing Administrators
importing_administrators_reform.description: When we came to these lands ready to fight, the thoughts of administering our conquests was an afterthought. Now, we have begun filling that gap with clerks and officials from our homeland. These new arrivals bring familiar ways with them.

relying_on_local_administrators_reform.name: Relying on Local Administrators
relying_on_local_administrators_reform.description: We came to these lands ready to shed blood and conquer in the name of our homeland. Now we are left with lands bereft of competent administrators. We have turned to the surviving locals to help administer these strange lands.

stake_our_claim.name: Stake Our Claim
stake_our_claim.description: "When this is all over, I'd like a little house in the woods, and all the gunpowder I can get my hands on." -Iacobb Creekwhistle\n\nEscann has suffered: its fields watered with blood and fertilized with ashes of the fallen. Today, we say no more. We did not fight this war to wipe out the orcs. We fought it to restore Escann. We must rebuild this land sooner rather than later, and welcome its people and any of those who wish to restore it with open arms.

end_the_greentide.name: End the Greentide
end_the_greentide.description: "There is war. Has been war. Will be war. But war for the sake of war means nothing. Wars must be fought to achieve peace." -Arosha Oakbreaker\n\nThe quest we signed up for did not change just because the Dookanson fell. The greenskin hordes, while beaten, continue to infest Escann, defiling and abusing it further with their mere presence. And worse, our brothers and sisters-in-arms have all but forgotten their purpose, settling the lands and falsifying titles without finishing what we started! The time of rebuilding is for a time of peace, but that time is not now. Not. Yet.

dwarovar_righting_the_oldest_grudge.name: Righting the Oldest Grudge
dwarovar_righting_the_oldest_grudge.description: We dwarves are an ancient people. We are a persevering people. We withstood the machinations of the giants and slew their greatest champions, and for a long time, we withstood the never-ending hordes of goblins and orcs that infested our home. It is our greatest failure as a people to allow these creatures to fester in our homes, and it is a great dishonour to our ancestors that for thousands and thousands of years we have not begun to reverse that shame until now. We must do our ancestors proud and do what must be done and not only drive these pests back into darkness, but to wipe them out completely.

dwarovar_treasures_of_our_forefathers.name: Treasures of Our Forefathers
dwarovar_treasures_of_our_forefathers.description: Few dwarves can even describe or imagine the power and fortune of our ancestors. It is said that massive gold hoards stretch from cavernous depth to cavernous ceiling, and even dragons could drown in even the most meagre vault. We have noble ambitions to take back what is rightfully ours. Our homes... and the treasures that lay waiting in it.

dwarovar_secrets_in_the_dark.name: Secrets in the Dark
dwarovar_secrets_in_the_dark.description: There are more than treasures and monsters waiting in the darkness of the Serpentspine. There are also ancient paths amidst the caves, which lead to both danger and opportunity. Let others scratch and squabble for the rocky surface. Our destiny lies deep below, among old knowledge and new possibilities.

converting_the_natives_reform.name: Converting the Natives
converting_the_natives_reform.description: In order to truly bring these lands under our control, we must first bring the natives into our fold. Through proper guidance, we have been able to turn the indigenous population from uncivilized brutes to truly accepted members of our society; both in faith and common practices.

salvation_through_work_reform.name: Salvation Through Work
salvation_through_work_reform.description: The uncouth masses we have conquered are not fit to direct their own lives. Instead, we must direct them, for their own good, in becoming productive members of society. While they may always remain unmannered and unlearned, we have set them upon work befitting their kind and bring salvation to them through the betterment of our society.

subservient_administrators_reform.name: Subservient Bureaucrats
subservient_administrators_reform.description: Our growing bureaucracy requires loyal officials who will do their duty to the state and trust that we have the best interests of the faith at heart.

religious_administrators_reform.name: Zealous Administrators
religious_administrators_reform.description: Religious conviction must be an integral part of our state in all its parts. When educating administrators among the clergy we must stress that they all get a solid grounding in theology so that they can take the right decisions at all times.

pious_merchants_reform.name: Mercantile Tithe
pious_merchants_reform.description: Spreading the one true faith is expensive work, and our merchants have heavy pockets that would prove useful to the faith if we were to elevate these rich men to positions of power within the state.

loyal_nobility_reform.name: Divine Nobility
loyal_nobility_reform.description: There are those in our realm who are born with divine blood; chosen by the holy forces of this world to lead and rule. Though the leader of our state is a non-hereditary position, we must elevate those born with divine blood to positions of leadership within our nation.

monastic_breweries_reform.name: Monastic Breweries
monastic_breweries_reform.description: The brewing of Mijiu is seen of paramount importance in Yinquan, as the drink is the favorite of the Huli-Jing and so the state pays Liho's Maidens richly for brewing it. A significant portion of the country's agricultural output has been set aside for the purposes of creating Mijiu, with the excess sold to peasants. With agriculture being treated as a commercial affair, output is well above what it would otherwise be.

warrior_monks_reform.name: Integration of the Sohei
warrior_monks_reform.description: Across the Eastern world, there are powerful orders of monks trained both in the ways of prayer and the martial arts. If we allow these monks to build temples within our lands, they would become an integral part of the state and greatly bolster the discipline and conviction of our armies.

anb_warrior_monks_reform.name: Warrior-Monks of the Xianjie
anb_warrior_monks_reform.description: The Xianjie is littered with powerful orders of monks trained both in the ways of meditation and the martial arts. These warrior-monks form the backbone of all Xia armies.

local_administration.name: Escanni Administration
local_administration.description: "'In the end'? Nothing ends, Corin. Nothing ever ends." -Nesterin the Gaunt\n\nThe Escanni were slaughtered — but some survived. The Escanni were crushed — but hope remained. And now that we are here to see that hope blossom into reality, those who kept it alive are our greatest resource. Exiled farmers know how best to work the land they were born in, refugee mayors know how best to run it, and banished nobles how to restore it to its old glory. With their guidance, Escann's revival is a certainty.

adventurer_administration.name: Adventurer Administration
adventurer_administration.description: "It's not where the hammer was forged that counts, it's how hard it hits!" -Rethag Ironbrow\n\nWe adventurers came from many places, for one cause: defeating the Greentide and restoring Escann. We've more or less done the first, but the same approach will be necessary for the second — and we'll need more recruits if we want to succeed, lest our blades dull and become complacent. Have the Tellumite criers put out that age-old call far and wide: adventurers wanted!

corintar_the_circle_reborn.name: The Circle Reborn
corintar_the_circle_reborn.description:
Near Corinsfall the poppies blow,
between the statues, row on row,
that mark our place; and in the sky,
our Mistress there lets out a sigh
scarce heard amidst the guns below.

We are her Circle. Short days ago
we lived, felt dawn, saw sunset glow,
loved and were loved, and now we lie,
near Corinsfall.

Take up our quarrel in her name.
To you from failing hands we throw
The torch; be yours to hold it high.
If ye break faith with us who die
We shall not sleep, though poppies grow
at Corinsfall.

corintar_warrior_priests.name: Warrior Priests
corintar_warrior_priests.description: A follower of Minara will not be chaste. A monk sworn to Begga will be charitable. And no one can call themselves a priest of the Goddess of War if they are not ready to fight in one. Given our government is filled with such priests, we are well-prepared for any and all conflicts to come.

dwarovar_hold_restoration.name: Hold Restoration
dwarovar_hold_restoration.description: The orcs and goblins have wronged us greatly, but it is folly for a dwarf to lose themselves in a quest for vengeance. We dwarves must look after ourselves first and foremost. In the shame of seeing our ancient homes smashed to rubble and left to rot it is difficult to see why we came here, but it is clear that it was not to simply kill orcs and goblins - it is to restore our old homes.

dwarovar_settlement_consolidation.name: Settlement Consolidation
dwarovar_settlement_consolidation.description: Restoring our ancient holds is a noble and important endeavour, but the long stagnation of the remnants has shown us that no hold can truly stand alone. If we want to repopulate the Dwarovar in earnest, then our people must expand beyond our lonesome walls, and we must connect our homes by road and rail to ensure that no foe can ever divide us again.

verdical_parliament.name: Verdical Parliament
verdical_parliament.description: Just as the High Rector holds ultimate authority over the religion and the state, the Verdicals who support our theological understandings also play a role in our administration

professional_bands_reform.name: Professional Bands
professional_bands_reform.description: The constant warfare and decentralized nature of our governing has led to the formation of many independent bands of soldiers. Well trained and organized, these bands of mercenaries make for a fine supplement to our traditional soldier caste.

cloistered_doctrines_reform.name: Cloistered Doctrines
cloistered_doctrines_reform.description: To survive the constant battles of our initial settlement of these lands, our rulers devised many novel tactics and strategies. These have become closely guarded secrets passed from officer to officer through the generations. The outstanding quality of our military leadership is proof that this hidden knowledge has become a key asset in our stewardship of these lands.

maintain_religious_head_reform.name: Strengthen Religious Head
maintain_religious_head_reform.description: We must strengthen the rule and abilities of our leader so that we can at all times be certain that the divine will is being carried out in accordance to their designs.

partial_secularisation_reform.name: Partial Secularisation
partial_secularisation_reform.description: Though religion will always be at the heart of our administration, some sectors of our government must be secularised if we are to adapt to this changing world.

battle_pope_reform.name: God Automata
battle_pope_reform.description: "Let me present you our latest invention, made especially for you, your Holiness. Imagine, if you will, a giant encased in a suit of armour, sanctified and divine, and capable of dealing holy justice upon your foes."\n\n(Yes, it's Mecha-High Rector)

conciliarism_reform.name: Embrace Conciliarism
conciliarism_reform.description: Conciliarism insists that it is the Global Council, not the High Rector, who holds supreme rulership over our country. By accepting this as truth in our faith and administration, we may vastly expand the power of the Veridicals and prevent the Papacy from falling under foreign influence.

crown_leader_reform.name: Crowned Ruler
crown_leader_reform.description: It is time we drop the trappings of religion and make our leader a monarch in own right.

proclaim_republic_reform.name: Proclaim Republic
proclaim_republic_reform.description: While we must never stray far from our religious roots it is also important that we allow our state to evolve. Let us create a free republic where the head of state is elected from among the group of valid candidates freely.

corintar_new_corintar.name: New Corintar
corintar_new_corintar.description: A chapter has closed. A new chapter has opened. The Great Purge is ended, and our administration must accommodate Corinites who have a slightly different (but no less passionate) vision of bringing about Her will.

found_our_nation.name: Become a Country
found_our_nation.description:
"You're something new, Corin. Something more." -Dominic of Gallowspeak to Corin, one week after the battle at Rottenstep

It is time. We came here as adventurers, but we've done that job twenty times over. We took back Escann's land, freed its people... but we have not restored it to its former glory. The chivalric kingdoms of old have all but fallen, its heirs slain and its legacy tarnished. But this is only the beginning. It has fallen to us to restore Escann to its glory, its pre-eminence. A bright future dawns for this war-torn land. For our company, no... for our country.

§T(Enables decisions to form your own country)§!

safeguard_holy_sites_reform.name: Safeguard Holy Sites
safeguard_holy_sites_reform.description: It is our holy duty to look after the holy pilgrimage sites under our control and to protect any pilgrims within our lands.

combat_heresy_reform.name: Combat Heresy
combat_heresy_reform.description: In the mission to spread the true faith we must never waver in our conviction and be ready to root out heresy wherever it springs up.

expel_heathens_reform.name: Expulsion of Heathens
expel_heathens_reform.description: As our state grows so does our exposure to wrongful teachings. We must not tolerate heathens or heretics within our walls. Instead these people can come live in our cities only if they convert to the true faith.

pursuit_of_knowledge_reform.name: Pursuit of Knowledge
pursuit_of_knowledge_reform.description: Our cause is not the defense of our religion nor the forceful expansion of the faith. Instead we focus on the very nature of being, the relationship of the mortal and the divine realms.

our_own_rulers_reform.name: Our own Rulers
our_own_rulers_reform.description: Long enough have we toiled in this foreign wild at the behest of the elite of our homeland. Risking the lives of our brothers and children for glory gained in a faraway city that reaps the spoils of our spilled blood. While once we may have been reliant on the aid of our sponsors, we now truly rule for ourselves. It is time our sovereignty is recognized at-large, for we are now our own masters!

gerudia_keep_dragon_aslumber.name: To Keep the Dragon Aslumber
gerudia_keep_dragon_aslumber.description: The foremost task of the Skalds is to ensure that the dread dragon Elkaesal the White remains aslumber. Nevermore will our land be covered in endless winter, we will uphold the legacy of the One-Handed Skald until our last breath!

clerical_commission_reform.name: Clerical Commission
clerical_commission_reform.description: We should divide the powers of the state between our religious leader and a commission of clerics. This commission will be charged with keeping the peace in our nation, and conducting diplomatic affairs on our leader’s behalf.

divine_guidance_reform.name: Divine Guidance
divine_guidance_reform.description: The people question if we should divide the power in our nation, but the power is already divided. The true leader of our state is divine in nature, and their will is interpreted by our leader here on Halann.

theocratic_democracy_reform.name: Theocratic Democracy
theocratic_democracy_reform.description: We are divinest of all, but we must never forget the inherent divinity found in even the lowliest of commoners. By allowing our peasants to elect their own representatives, we allow them to have their own voice in the court of the divine.

constitional_theocracy_reform.name: Constitutional Theocracy
constitional_theocracy_reform.description: The state exists for the sole purpose to serve the divine. But to be completely devoted to this purpose we must ensure that no weak-minded theocratic ruler can come to power to corrupt our cause.

regionally_elected_commanders.name: Regionally Elected Commanders
regionally_elected_commanders.description: In our government, the successor to the current ruler is decided by a vote from the warriors who have dedicated their lives to protecting the Faith. By allowing the citizens of the towns and villages under our protection to partake in electing the Commander that represents their interests, we may begin to expand our administration and make those under our protection feel accurately represented in the Court of Commanders.

monastic_elections_reform.name: Open Public Elections
monastic_elections_reform.description: Many villages under our protection demand representation, and although they are fools who do not understand that our rulership is divine, we must grant them some concessions if we are to keep them happy. Traditionally, only the elite of the Clergy would vote for the new leader of our government, but by opening elections to the public and establishing two new subdivisions of our government for the people to vote for, they may feel their interests are represented by the state.

commander_king_reform.name: A Dynastic Theocracy
commander_king_reform.description: Faith will always play a vital role in our state, but it is becoming clear that our government is not adaptable enough to rule vast swathes of land and function in this changing world. There is no reason that our men of faith must swear themselves into celibacy and by reorganizing our state into a Dynastic Theocracy and granting our rulers royal titles, we will become protectors of the faith and the crown, guided by our Theocratic King.

admiral_king_reform.name: Lords of the Sea
admiral_king_reform.description: The good work our soldiers have done in securing safe passage for pilgrims across the sea has made our sails a feared sight for pirates and heathens alike. However, it has become clear that the threat pirates pose to our faith go far beyond our small shores, and we must expand our government to become an Empire in itself if we are to properly serve our holy mission. By enacting a series of reforms within our government, we shall vastly expand our administration beyond our borders and our valiant Knights will elect all future commanders based on the number of pirate lords they have brought to kneel before God!

church_and_state_reform.name: Church and State
church_and_state_reform.description: The faith and the state are the two pillars that hold up the world. Our laws are holy laws and they cannot be questioned.

god_and_man_reform.name: God and Man
god_and_man_reform.description: The only relationship that matters is that between lowly mortals and the immortal holy spirits. Our state exists merely to remind people of the proper ways to devote themselves to prayer.

religious_harmony_reform.name: Soul and Body
religious_harmony_reform.description: We must never forget that the barriers of culture and language are merely an illusion. We are all mere souls inhabiting a shell. It does not matter what language we speak, only that we service the divine in the proper way.

faith_and_power_reform.name: Faith and Power
faith_and_power_reform.description: Combat and power are the only ways to come closer to divine. Through victories on the battlefield, the people of our state show their piety and devotion.

belief_and_unity_reform.name: Belief and Unity
belief_and_unity_reform.description: Faith is the unifying power which keeps the state and the people together. The government exists for the mere purpose to ensure that this unity remains unbroken.

religious_culture_reform.name: Religious Society
religious_culture_reform.description: Religion and culture are intertwined. No man of our society can claim to be of our culture if they do not follow the same rituals.

harmonization_of_cultures_reform.name: Harmonization of Cultures
harmonization_of_cultures_reform.description: The concept of culture is in many ways similar to religion, though it is forged by traditions, social values and environment and not by divine enlightenment. The cultures within our state are bound to harmonize with each other as long they are following the true faith.

cultural_safe_haven_reform.name: Cultural Safe Haven
cultural_safe_haven_reform.description: Cultures are fabricated social norms, created by men to differentiate them from others. In actuality, we are all mortal bodies walking on this world. Only the conviction to the true divine being is of importance.

taxation_of_the_foreigners_reform.name: Taxation of Foreigners
taxation_of_the_foreigners_reform.description: The culture of minorities is of little concern to us. As long they are willing to sacrifice part of their income for the holy cause, they are free to live out their traditions.

religion_in_one_country_reform.name: One State Under God
religion_in_one_country_reform.description: Our faith and our state have become totally synonymous with each other. We are our religion, and any who devote themselves to the divine, must also devote themselves to the state.

religious_permanent_revolution_reform.name: The Global Crusade
religious_permanent_revolution_reform.description: This world is full of heretics, heathens, sinners, and blasphemers! We must seek to correct heretics and heathens alike, and spread our peaceful ways through force!

many_fingers_of_god_reform.name: The Many Fingers of God
many_fingers_of_god_reform.description: We believe that there is only one God, by whom we live and by whom we die, and for whom we have an upright heart. But as God gives us the different fingers of the hand, so he gives to men diverse ways to approach him.

priestly_autonomy_reform.name: Priestly Autonomy
priestly_autonomy_reform.description: No priest should hold power over another priest, with the exception of our ruler. Each realm shall be fully autonomous, and priests will remain free to interpret the will of the Gods in any way that is true to them.

state_of_theologians_reform.name: State of Theologians
state_of_theologians_reform.description: Our religion is no weapon or justification of war. Nor does it exist for monetary sake. Our faith is the result of intellectual and spiritual theories, discussions and understanding. The theology is the purpose of the state and its subjects.

divinity_through_craftsmanship_reform.name: Divinity through Craftsmanship
divinity_through_craftsmanship_reform.description: The theologians discuss the means of reaching divine enlightenment, but only few realize what truly brings us closer to the immortal realm. The act of creation is the only holy way to approach our maker.

sacred_elven_empire_reform.name: Sacred Elven Empire
sacred_elven_empire_reform.description: The followers of Dahvar have reorganized the traditional Aelnar monarchy into a theocracy. While a nominal royal family still exists, the Nùr Dhavaraesa church holds true power within the state.

beatific_birth_reform.name: Beatific Birth
beatific_birth_reform.description: Giving birth is a sacred duty of any star elf

sacred_body_reform.name: Sacred Body
sacred_body_reform.description: Elven body are temple, and should be preserved as much as possible, even on the battlefield

administration_in_the_clergy_reform.name: Administration in the Clergy
administration_in_the_clergy_reform.description: Clergy in the Administration ? No, Administration in the Clergy

godhood_reform.name: Godhood
godhood_reform.description: Every living elf is a god among ants. Every dead elf is a star in a constellation

black_demesne_reform.name: The Black Demesne
black_demesne_reform.description: When the Ascendant Dragon rose to the Skull Throne, order was forged from the fundamental chaos of the Prime Discordance -- a plan to change the world. In the Demesne, it is magical ability and mystic intuition that mark a person's worth, not their bloodline. Magic users are full citizens of a harsh but mostly fair state. On the other hand, non-magicians live a life of total legal subjugation and suppression, though some manage to accrue considerable influence nonetheless.

black_acolyte_reform.name: Black Acolyte
black_acolyte_reform.description: Excessive centralization creates vulnerabilities, and is unbecoming of a nation built on the chaotic flow of the Prime Discordance. As such, powerful mages known as Acolytes are selected to work closely with the central government. The subsequent competition for favor breeds powerful, devious mages who can carry on the work of building a new world from the ruins of the old.

black_hands_reform.name: Black Hands
black_hands_reform.description: Along with being the direct apprentices of the Sorcerer-King, the Acolytes receive the right to administer parts of the Demesne, where their will shall be law. This system of decentralized government that is still controlled by personal bonds of loyalty and admiration is part of the genius of the Black Demesne: it is chaos controlled.

the_black_blueprint_reform.name: The Black Blueprint
the_black_blueprint_reform.description: We know how things must be, but our will cannot yet accomplish it. This is the central problem that drives one to study magic in the first place. Like the magician, the Demesne must undergo a transformation so that it might enact its will across all Cannor. To achieve this, we have created both an internal and external plan; a plan for learning and a plan for doing.

tehanek_magocracy_reform.name: Council of the Mage-Shamans
tehanek_magocracy_reform.description: The government is run by wise mage-shamans.

pelimkana_kalemba_reform.name: Kalemba
pelimkana_kalemba_reform.description: Pelimkana has long been under the control of the Order of Pelimkana, and has rejected the Tengalam system of monarchy of the Yanglam. The resulting state is exceedingly isolationist and particularly zealous, even disallowing logging in the Lupulan. The blessings of the spirits have made their lands exceedingly productive as a result, though this comes at the cost of foreign trade.

chaingrasper_dakocracy_reform.name: Dakocracy
chaingrasper_dakocracy_reform.description: A mage-dominated society centralized under its ruler. The ruler has complete control over every aspect of society.

chaingrasper_early_dakocracy_reform.name: Early Dakocracy
chaingrasper_early_dakocracy_reform.description: A mage-dominated society centralized under its ruler. The ruler tries to gain complete control over every aspect of society.

gemradcurt_order_run_arcane_education_re.name: Order-Run Arcane Education
gemradcurt_order_run_arcane_education_re.description: With many of its other responsibilities transferred to the Eiganrac, the Taigan Order has once more dedicated itself to training the mages of Gemradcurt. Taking any boy or girl with magical potential into their ranks, the Order has now focused much of its efforts on turning them into loyal and powerful agents of Immarel's will.

gemradcurt_ice_grip_over_state_reform.name: Ice-Grip over the State
gemradcurt_ice_grip_over_state_reform.description: Following a series of widespread purges, and the implementation of new restrictions on various organisations outside the state's official control, Immarel has solidified her ice grip over the state even further. Though the elites of Gemradcurt are still mostly free to go about their business, the regime is now much better equipped to deal with those who step out of line.

bulwari_temple_reform.name: Bulwari Temple
bulwari_temple_reform.description: Bulwari temples are the house of Surael, a place of worship, but they are also massive complexes with schools, workshops, storehouses and libraries. The kašra is the religious, as well as political, leader of the temple, while the šasig, or head administrator, is in charge of the day-to-day functioning of the temple and its institutions.

earthly_mission.name: Earthly Mission
earthly_mission.description: Our gods are the very earth we walk on, the very air we breathe, the clothes we wear and the fire we warm ourselves by. We should dedicate ourselves to improving the physical world around us through charity and empathy.

cultic_tolerance.name: Cultic Tolerance
cultic_tolerance.description: As our cults have adopted practices from the various religions of Halann, we have gained a great deal of respect for these people and their traditions. We should look to them as teachers and guides, incorporating their knowledge into our own.

education_of_the_military.name: Education of the Military
education_of_the_military.description: Our religion is very mutlifaceted with many different gods. Our soldiers come from different clans, and most likely worship different gods. This might cause internal strife, despite believing in the same pantheon. We need to educate them about all of our gods, so they may fight together with greater unity for our gods and eachother.

bronze_stratocracy_reform.name: Bronze Stratocracy
bronze_stratocracy_reform.description: Devised by Aakhet himself as a way to keep order in Aramoole and ensure his will is done. A new ruler is selected from amongst the top generals in the country when the previous one either passes away or is considered incapable of fulfilling the role anymore. Most top generals are battlemages who, while far below the level of the most powerful mages, are extremely skilled at weilding their limited magical prowess in battle.

raja_magocracy_reform.name: Mahyaraja
raja_magocracy_reform.description: The Raj is malleable, and it is the duty of the Mahyaraja to unwind its innards, boil out its pus, grind down its bones, and form it anew amidst the dissent and anguish of the oppressed. In the end, the upper castes may protest, but we shall be the liberators of the lowly. After all, suffering is temporary, but the Raj shall be eternal.

vf_krilavuna_magocracy.name: The Krilavuna
vf_krilavuna_magocracy.description: An evolved form of the old Symposium, the "Blood Crown" passes between Vels Fadhecai's most prodigious mages, who each receive exclusive tutulage from Lady Mitrenya... whenever she can be bothered, that is.

spider_silk_order.name: Silk Order
spider_silk_order.description:
A religious order based on the distribution of spider-goods. Spidergoods being seen as a physical representation of Muta Kroozpauz' realm. The shamans are the primary authority on most matters regarding trade, faith and production. 
 
§YThings that affect Caravan Preparation:§! 
Negative Diplomatic Reputation: §R-0.2§! Monthly Caravan Preparation 
Average Local Autonomy from 20% to above 50%: §R-0.05 to -0.4§! Monthly Caravan Preparation 
Per Center of Trade level 2 or 3 with a §YMarketplace§!: §G+0.015 to 0.03§! Monthly Caravan Preparation (Capped at §G+0.3§!) 
Per Silk Manufactory: §G+0.02§! Monthly Caravan Preparation (Capped at §G+0.2§!)
Being at Peace: §G0.33§! Monthly Caravan Preparation 
Stability: §R-0.24§! to §G+0.24§! Monthly Caravan Preparation

spiderwretch_T2_spider_warfare.name: Ensnaring the Plains
spiderwretch_T2_spider_warfare.description: Our nation, unlike most others, extensively use spiders in all matters, from military to production to even beverages. By implementing centaur ideas and tactics into our government and military, we can learn so much more than previously thought. Although we beat them, their knowlegde is still useful in controlling the plains.

eagle_hobgoblins_searching_reform.name: In Search of Order
eagle_hobgoblins_searching_reform.description: When the Day of Ashen Skies occurred, chaos overpowered order, bringing imbalance to the world. It is imperative we unite our people and help restore balance between the powers of chaos and order.\n\nOur ruler may have proved themselves martially, but their focus must be on enlightenment. They shall use their magic only to seek out Order, bring it to their people, and in waging the war against Chaos.

eagle_hobgoblins_rule_of_order_reform.name: Rule of Order
eagle_hobgoblins_rule_of_order_reform.description: To rule is to bring Order to the world, and repel the Chaos that was unleashed. To achieve such a feat is a difficult task, and only our strongest soldier of Order should lead us. As such, any would be ruler must prove their strength above all others in a trial of combat, for if one cannot best their fellow mages, how can they possibly stand against Chaos?

abyssal_otardom_reform.name: Abyssal Otardom
abyssal_otardom_reform.description: "It has proven difficult to trace the origins of the common name for the beastly collective - the 'Abyssal Otardom'. I have successfully pinpointed that it likely arose amongst the humans of either Volnimestin or Bal Ouord, who engaged in joint correspondence with the surrounding Bahari states. It is my belief, therefore, that the name was an attempt to dramatise the monstrous horde and impress the need for assistance more effectively. As we know, this attempt failed, but the descriptor has persisted." -Unknown Gelkari Chronicler.

otaric_succession_reform.name: Otaric Succession
otaric_succession_reform.description: "When an Otar perishes (despite popular belief, they can, in fact, die), a brief period of animalistic anarchy can be observed amongst the beasts, even in their most distant holdings. This usually calms following some kind of ritualistic combat deep within the mountain, with a new Otar taking the position. Interestingly, some of these Otars have instead taken the title 'Madar' for reasons we have yet to determine - this epithet however, has not caught on amongst their subjects, or the general public." -Unknown Gelkari Chronicler.

rights_razorblessed_reform.name: Rights Of The Razorblessed
rights_razorblessed_reform.description: "The beasts appear unnaturally skilled at controlling the borders of their territory, but are even better at impressing their sick beliefs upon the vulnerable. Their brand of evangelism is extremely singular - they break a given population down until hope is totally extinguished, before allowing them the freedom to determine their new philosophies. Via some unknown phenomenon, those philosophies universally surround some 'god-begot'; an enigmatic being, likely connected with the Moon. As a result, those who attempt to escape are stopped, and usually punished quite severely, whilst many never make the attempt in the first place." -Unknown Gelkari Chronicler.

floodborn_council_reform_theocracy.name: Floodborn Council
floodborn_council_reform_theocracy.description: The Floodborn of Wyvernheart have been assembled into a grand council which directs the nation's administration and votes on decisions to present to the Sovereign who has the final say in all matters.

raheni_divine_monarchy_reform.name: Raheni Divine Monarchy
raheni_divine_monarchy_reform.description: With a burning zeal fuelled by Surael's light, the Monarchs of Suryavar have dedicated their realm and people to spreading the word of truth; that of the Jadd, the light, that which would cast darkness out of Rahen and beyond.

peasants_theocracy_reform.name: Peasants Theocracy
peasants_theocracy_reform.description: Rejecting mages and kings, Corinsfield also rejects the rigid hierarchy present in many theocracies. While only Corinite couters can hold positions of power, there are no restrictions on becoming a couter besides the nomination of the prior. While this does seem as if it would lead to nepotism, such as a couter nominating their own family, this is held at bay by the strict social taboos of their community. The Crimson Reverend must be chosen by two-thirds of the couters, and from there they must be able to recite five Corinite hymns from memory.

blessed_literacy_reform.name: Corinite Blessed Education
blessed_literacy_reform.description: The robust schooling system of Corinsfield ensures that much of the population is literate - a rarity for many nations at this time. This also ensures good stock for the government; not only are the majority of couters in the assembly guaranteed to have a good education, but to also embody righteousness and faith. However, as lands in the Corinsfield core oft have more resources to fund schools in comparison to more recently conquered lands, it leads to them more often than not dominating the political conversation.

peasant_councils_reform.name: Farmer Councils
peasant_councils_reform.description: The Corinspoppy Compact enshrines the governmental structure of Corinsfield. Every town is organized into councils of farmers, who take grievances to their local couters. On the 18th day of every month, couters are to meet in Poppyspring to speak of what their communities require. From there, the assembly votes - a new law or state action requires a two-thirds majority, and the presiding Crimson Reverend has the ability to veto laws they view as unjust or blasphemous. Every town must be represented before the beginning of a meeting, and towns are permitted to send a replacement in the event that their couter cannot attend.

az_an_gov_reform_high_priesthood_of_hala.name: High Priesthood of Halanna
az_an_gov_reform_high_priesthood_of_hala.description: The reclaiming dwarves who took authority over the ruined places of Ovdal-az-Ân quickly consolidated around major ruined religious sites, which by the restoration of the Dwarven Pantheon had formed powerful priestly bastions, each dedicated to a different deity and dominating a neighbourhood the size of some surface cities.\n\nOf all such temple precincts, the cathedral-complex of Argrâstun is the most prestigious, the heart of government and the place where the High Priest of Halanna is elected. The High Priest is formally granted absolute power to defend dwarven tradition and religion, though in practice temple-bureaucracies wield considerable influence in their own right.

exemplar_state.name: The Exemplar State
exemplar_state.description: The state is ruled by the Holy Orders, with the Council of Heralds under the Herald of Light at its apex. Most internal affairs and low-level administrative tasks are managed by the governing bodies of the individual city states, with the Holy Orders acting as impartial arbitrators and overseers, but all fundamental policy decisions, particularly those regarding military and foreign policy, are dictated by the Herald of Light.

mission_of_exemplary.name: Mission Of Exemplary
mission_of_exemplary.description: The Duty of the Exemplars is to serve as examples of righteousness to all the people under their sway, especially if they do not yet have let the Light of Surael into their hearts and minds and still cling to their misguided superstitions or agnostic delusions. Even they shall, in due time, find their way to the truth, while we protect them from harm. Our virtuous conduct shall speak more eloquently of the rectitude of our cause than any proselytizing prayer can hope to be.

yuanszi_xia_reform.name: Heaven-Sent Surgeon
yuanszi_xia_reform.description: We were sent by the heavens to truly heal the sickness that threatens Haless by any means necessary, any. We will not be kind, we will not be soft, for tender healers cause wounds to fester and rot. We will cut out the organs of blindness and amputate the limbs of weakness. Haless will know pain and blood at the hands of the Heaven-Sent Surgeon, but she will live more than ever before.

ekxiem_zokka.name: Ekxiem Zokka
ekxiem_zokka.description: There is beauty in its simplicity: Zokka is the pack, and the pack is Zokka. Zokka will succeed in his goal. Without Zokka, there would be no pack. Without his hunger, there would be no Zokka. Power is earned through competence and loyalty above all else, and the pack is stronger for it.\n\nThere is a catch. Without the First Sun, there would be no hunger. Without his hunger, there would be no Zokka. Without Zokka, there would be no pack. But Zokka will succeed in his goal. The pack know that, one day, their world will end - a tragic side-effect of their Lord's ambition. To serve is to commit to that ending. As a consequence, only the most committed, serve.

ekxiem_zokka_heirs.name: Ekxiem Zokka
ekxiem_zokka_heirs.description: There is beauty in its simplicity: Zokka is the pack, and the pack is Zokka. Zokka will succeed in his goal. Without Zokka, there would be no pack. Without his hunger, there would be no Zokka. Power is earned through competence and loyalty above all else, and the pack is stronger for it.\n\nThere is a catch. Without the First Sun, there would be no hunger. Without his hunger, there would be no Zokka. Without Zokka, there would be no pack. But Zokka will succeed in his goal. The pack know that, one day, their world will end - a tragic side-effect of their Lord's ambition. To serve is to commit to that ending. As a consequence, only the most committed, serve.

zokka_hand_feeds.name: The Hand That Feeds
zokka_hand_feeds.description: The Pack must grow. Humans carry with them new thought and knowledge, diverse and connected as they are. This is a form of fire, and fire must be permitted to spread.

zokka_mouth_eats.name: The Mouth That Eats
zokka_mouth_eats.description: The Pack must eat. Let the weak burn, and their ruins will nourish the strong. After the First Sun is dead, they will thank us for allowing them to play a small part.

zokka_immoveable_kult.name: An Immoveable Kult
zokka_immoveable_kult.description: Truth is a still, solid thing. Those who bow to the flame will be allowed to stand beside us, so long as they bow willingly.

zokka_unstoppable_kult.name: An Unstoppable Kult
zokka_unstoppable_kult.description: A wildfire is just so - wild, and indiscriminate. Those who refuse to howl our creed will scream it with their dying breath!

zokka_paramountcy.name: Paramountcy
zokka_paramountcy.description: Panic is poison to lesser minds. The end of Halann is coming, yes - but let the Pack's leaders be the ones to deal with that. We will decide when the rest need to know.

zokka_perdition.name: Perdition
zokka_perdition.description: The end of Halann is a necessary consequence of Zokka's Great Work. We have accepted this truth, but many of our new subjects reject it outright. This cannot be allowed to continue - not whilst the First Sun still shines.

zokka_hand_feeds2.name: The Hand That Feeds
zokka_hand_feeds2.description: The Pack must grow. Humans carry with them new thought and knowledge, diverse and connected as they are. This is a form of fire, and fire must be permitted to spread.

zokka_mouth_eats2.name: The Mouth That Eats
zokka_mouth_eats2.description: The Pack must eat. Let the weak burn, and their ruins will nourish the strong. After the First Sun is dead, they will thank us for allowing them to play a small part.

zokka_immoveable_kult2.name: An Immoveable Kult
zokka_immoveable_kult2.description: Truth is a still, solid thing. Those who bow to the flame will be allowed to stand beside us, so long as they bow willingly.

zokka_unstoppable_kult2.name: An Unstoppable Kult
zokka_unstoppable_kult2.description: A wildfire is just so - wild, and indiscriminate. Those who refuse to howl our creed will scream it with their dying breath!

zokka_paramountcy2.name: Paramountcy
zokka_paramountcy2.description: Panic is poison to lesser minds. The end of Halann is coming, yes - but let the Pack's leaders be the ones to deal with that. We will decide when the rest need to know.

zokka_perdition2.name: Perdition
zokka_perdition2.description: The end of Halann is a necessary consequence of Zokka's Great Work. We have accepted this truth, but many of our new subjects reject it outright. This cannot be allowed to continue - not whilst the First Sun still shines.

steppe_horde.name: Steppe Nomads
steppe_horde.description: A governmental form where a sovereign or military ruler rule a tribal chiefdom, with constant Casus Bellis on all their neighbors and troops who fight better on the plains.

tribal_federation.name: Tribal Federation
tribal_federation.description: A governmental form where a confederation of tribes rule with a certain degree of internal autonomy but are nationally governed by a central authority.

tribal_despotism.name: Tribal Despotism
tribal_despotism.description: A political system where a tribe is controlled by an individual ruler with unlimited power and the ability to exercise any action without consequence or retribution, often ruling through fear.

tribal_kingdom.name: Tribal Monarchy
tribal_kingdom.description: A political system where a large tribal society has formed a kingdom, with one strong ruler who rules through law.

polynesian_tribe.name: Polynesian Tribe
